senses.definition: a small pointy piece of metal that can be fasten into wooden or metal objects using a screwdriver to hold things together

examples.x: He used a screwdriver to tighten the screws holding the shelves to the wall.
The carpenter inserted screws into the wooden boards to construct the frame.
She tightened the screws on the chair to prevent it from wobbling.
The technician used a screwdriver to remove the screws holding the panel in place.
He replaced the old screws with longer ones to better secure the shelf to the wall.

senses.definition: a piece of metal like a thick nail without a point that used to secure assembled parts by passing through holes and tightening with a nut

examples.x: I need to tighten the bolt on the bicycle wheel.
Make sure to secure the furniture to the wall with bolts.
Please hand me the wrench so I can loosen the bolt.
The construction worker used a heavy-duty bolt to connect the steel beams.
The mechanic replaced the rusted bolt with a new one.

senses.definition: a fastener with a spring-loaded wing or toggle mechanism used for secure anchoring of heavy objects to walls or ceilings

examples.x: To hang the heavy mirror on the drywall, I used a toggle bolt for added support.
The toggle bolt held the shelf firmly in place, even though the wall was hollow.
When installing the curtain rod, we used toggle bolts to secure it to the ceiling.
The contractor recommended using toggle bolts to fasten the heavy brackets to the thin walls.
I had to use a toggle bolt because the wall was too thin for regular screws.

senses.definition: a threaded fastener with a pointed tip and helical threads along its shank, specifically designed for securely joining pieces of wood together by creating a threaded hole in the wood as it is driven in, offering strong and reliable fastening in woodworking projects
a threaded fastener designed to be used with a nut or threaded hole in metal or other materials, commonly used for fastening machine components or parts together in various industrial applications

examples.x: The technician used a machine screw to attach the metal plate to the frame.
To secure the components, he tightened a machine screw with a wrench.
The assembly required several machine screws to fasten the parts together.
She needed a machine screw with a finer thread to ensure a tight fit in the small hole.
The engineer checked the machine screws to make sure they were properly tightened before testing the equipment.

senses.definition: a special screw used for attaching thin metal sheets together, with sharp threads for a strong grip, commonly used in metalwork, construction, cars, and electrical projects

a threaded fastener with a sharp tip and specially designed threads that allow it to create its own threaded hole in a material, eliminating the need for a pre-drilled hole and enabling efficient and convenient installation in various applications

examples.x: The carpenter used a self-tapping screw to attach the metal brackets to the wooden frame.
For the project, you will need self-tapping screws to fasten the metal panels together.
He quickly installed the shelf by driving a few self-tapping screws into the wall.
The technician used a self-tapping screw to connect the electrical components inside the box.
When assembling the metal structure, she preferred using self-tapping screws for their ease and speed.

senses.definition: a specialized type of self-tapping screw with a coarse thread and a sharp point, specifically designed for fastening drywall panels to wood or metal studs during construction or renovation projects

examples.x: To ensure a secure fit, he drove the drywall screw deep into the wall frame.
She tightened the drywall screw until its head was flush with the surface.
The worker used a drywall screw to attach the panel to the wooden stud.
Make sure to use a power drill when installing drywall screws for quicker work.
The contractor recommended using drywall screws instead of nails for a stronger hold.

senses.definition: a type of fastener specifically designed for attaching objects to concrete or masonry surfaces

examples.x: To secure the shelf to the wall, I used concrete screws to anchor it firmly.
You will need a drill to create a hole before inserting the concrete screw into the wall.
I had to buy a set of concrete screws to hang the heavy mirror on the basement wall.
The concrete screws held the brackets in place even under the weight of the heavy equipment.
After drilling the proper holes, I carefully screwed in the concrete screws to mount the shelf.

senses.definition: a strong wooden screw with a pointed end and rough threads, used to securely attach things like wood or metal together

examples.x: The carpenter used a lag screw to secure the heavy beam to the wall.
To prevent the deck from shifting, the builder attached the posts with lag screws.
The lag screw provided a strong and stable connection between the wooden planks.
When assembling the wooden frame, she tightened each lag screw carefully to ensure it held securely.

senses.definition: a type of wood screw specifically designed for outdoor decking applications

examples.x: The contractor used deck screws to secure the wooden planks to the frame of the new deck.
Make sure to choose rust-resistant deck screws for outdoor projects to avoid corrosion.
I needed to buy more deck screws to finish installing the railing on the deck.
We picked up some deck screws at the hardware store to finish our backyard deck.
When building a deck, always use deck screws to ensure a sturdy, long-lasting structure.

senses.definition: a type of specialized wood screw designed for use with particle board or other engineered wood products

examples.x: To assemble the bookshelf, use particle board screws to attach the sides to the back panel.
I need to buy a pack of particle board screws to fix the loose piece of my desk.
The carpenter recommended using particle board screws for the project, as they provide a better hold in softwood materials.
The instructions say to use particle board screws for the frame to ensure a strong, stable connection.
When building furniture, particle board screws are essential for ensuring the pieces stay tightly together.

senses.definition: a specialized fastener designed to prevent unauthorized removal or tampering

examples.x: The security screw on the vending machine ensures that only authorized personnel can open it.
The technicians used a special tool to remove the security screws from the back of the device.
The playground equipment was installed with security screws to prevent theft or damage.
To access the inner workings of the server, we had to remove the security screws using a unique screwdriver.
The public restroom stalls were fitted with security screws to discourage vandalism.

senses.definition: a screw with a circular loop or "eye" at the end instead of a traditional head

examples.x: The carpenter used an eye screw to hang the heavy mirror on the wall.
She attached a rope to the eye screw to secure the tent in place during the storm.
The eye screw on the ceiling was perfect for hanging the chandelier.
They used several eye screws to attach the swing to the tree branches.
The eye screw in the boat allowed the sailor to tie down the anchor securely.

senses.definition: a type of screw that features two large wings or extended knobs on the head, allowing for easy manual tightening and loosening without the need for tools

examples.x: The wing screw made it easy to adjust the height of the chair without needing any tools.
She tightened the wing screw by hand to secure the panel in place.
The technician removed the wing screw to access the inner components of the machine.
I prefer using wing screws for DIY projects since they allow for tool-free adjustments.

senses.definition: a fastener with a hexagonal-shaped head, commonly tightened or loosened using a wrench or socket

examples.x: The mechanic used a hex head screw to secure the parts of the engine.
To assemble the table, I needed to use a hex head screw to attach the legs.
The technician tightened the hex head screw on the machine to prevent any loose parts.
Hex head screws are often used in outdoor furniture because of their durability.
To repair the car, the mechanic removed the hex head screws holding the panel in place.

senses.definition: a type of fastener that features a square-shaped socket or recess on the screw head

examples.x: I prefer square drive screws because they are less likely to slip out during installation.
The carpenter used a square drive screw to fasten the wooden boards securely.
He reached for a square drive screw to fix the metal brackets to the wall.
For the outdoor project, the builder used rust-resistant square drive screws for added durability.

senses.definition: a unique fastener that can only be installed in one direction, providing a level of security and preventing easy removal

examples.x: The security system was installed using one-way screws to prevent tampering.
The access panel was secured with one-way screws, making it difficult to open without the right tools.
One-way screws are often used in public spaces to keep machinery and devices safe from unauthorized adjustments.
The manufacturer used one-way screws to secure the battery compartment of the toy, ensuring children could not open it.

senses.definition: a type of fastener designed with a large, knurled or textured head that can be easily tightened or loosened by hand without the need for additional tools

examples.x: The technician used a thumb screw to open the case of the computer without needing any tools.
He tightened the thumb screw on the shelf to secure it in place.
The repair manual recommended using a thumb screw to hold the panel in position.
You can adjust the height of the chair by turning the thumb screw on the side.

senses.definition: a type of fastener that is designed to remain attached to the panel or component it is securing, even when fully loosened

examples.x: The technician used a captive panel screw to secure the cover, making it easier to access the components inside.
After removing the panel, the captive panel screw stayed in place, making it easier to put the panel back without searching for the screw.
The maintenance team prefers captive panel screws because they prevent the screws from getting lost during routine checks.
The server racks are held together with captive panel screws, ensuring the panels stay attached while allowing for quick access.

senses.definition: a type of screw with a cross-shaped indentation on the head that corresponds to a Phillips screwdriver, providing a secure and convenient fastening method in various applications

examples.x: The technician used a Phillips screw to secure the panel, ensuring it stayed firmly in place.

senses.definition: a type of screw that features a Pozidriv head, which has a cross-shaped indentation with additional smaller indents, allowing for improved torque transmission and reduced risk of slippage during tightening or loosening operations

examples.x: The carpenter used a Pozidriv screw to secure the wooden panels in place.
When assembling the furniture, make sure to use a Pozidriv screw for a better grip.
The technician preferred the Pozidriv screw for its durability and ease of use.
For this project, a Pozidriv screw is recommended to prevent slipping during installation.
The mechanic chose a Pozidriv screw to ensure the parts stayed securely attached.

senses.definition: a specialized fastener with a unique head design, requiring specific tools for installation and removal, to deter unauthorized tampering or removal

examples.x: The tamper-resistant screws were a necessary addition to the ATM, making it harder to tamper with.
To ensure the equipment stays safe, they installed tamper-resistant screws on all the outer panels.
The security system was designed with tamper-resistant screws to deter any attempts at breaking in.
Workers had to use a special tool to remove the tamper-resistant screws during the maintenance check.

senses.definition: a fastener with threaded ends on both sides, designed to be inserted into pre-drilled holes and used to join two pieces of wood or other materials together securely

examples.x: To join the two wooden boards securely, we used a dowel screw for a strong connection.
The carpenter inserted the dowel screw into the pre-drilled holes to attach the legs to the table.
For the bookshelf project, we decided to use dowel screws to ensure a clean, seamless look.
The wooden frame was put together using dowel screws, providing a sturdy and hidden connection.
The two pieces of wood were joined together with a dowel screw, which provided a tight, secure hold.

senses.definition: a type of screw that features a head with three triangular wings, requiring a Tri-Wing screwdriver or bit for proper installation or removal

examples.x: To prevent unauthorized access, the device was secured with tri-wing screws.
The tri-wing screw was difficult to remove without the special screwdriver designed for it.
A tri-wing screw held the back panel of the camera, making it harder to tamper with.
The manufacturer used tri-wing screws in the assembly of the remote control to prevent easy disassembly.

senses.definition: a screw with a star-shaped head that requires a corresponding Torx screwdriver or bit for proper use

examples.x: The mechanic used a Torx screw to assemble the car‚Äôs engine parts securely.
The design of the Torx screw prevents the tool from slipping out during use.

senses.definition: a type of screw that features a unique head with two holes or recesses, requiring a spanner screwdriver or bit with a two-pin tip for proper installation or removal

examples.x: The security system in the building uses snake eye screws to prevent tampering with the equipment.
To protect the machinery, the company decided to install snake eye screws in all the important components.
The new outdoor sign is fixed with snake eye screws to avoid theft or vandalism.
The technician had to use a special tool to remove the snake eye screws during the maintenance check.
The vending machine had snake eye screws to prevent anyone from stealing the money inside.

senses.definition: a fastener with a threaded post and a screw head, commonly used for binding or securing materials

examples.x: The designer used a Chicago screw to attach the leather straps to the bag, ensuring a secure yet removable connection.
The bookbinder replaced the damaged binding with a Chicago screw for a durable and adjustable fastening.

senses.definition: a small tool with a metal part by which screws can be turned

examples.x: He grabbed a screwdriver to assemble the new furniture.
The set included different types of screwdrivers for various screw heads.
He kept a screwdriver in his toolbox for quick repairs around the house.
The magnetic tip of the screwdriver helped hold screws in place.

senses.definition: a tool with a flat, straight blade used for turning screws with a corresponding slot or "flathead" screw head

examples.x: He used a flathead screwdriver to tighten the loose screw on the door handle.
The flathead screwdriver slipped out of the screw, making the job more difficult.
She grabbed a flathead screwdriver from the toolbox to fix the loose hinge.
He carefully turned the flathead screwdriver to secure the screws in the wooden panel.
The technician needed a flathead screwdriver to adjust the screws on the machine.

senses.definition: a tool with a cross-shaped tip used for turning screws with a corresponding Phillips head, allowing for efficient tightening or loosening of screws in various applications

examples.x: She used a Phillips screwdriver to tighten the screws on the cabinet door.
I need a Phillips screwdriver to assemble the furniture I bought.
The technician grabbed a Phillips screwdriver to open the back panel of the device.
If you have a Phillips screwdriver, the job will go faster.
I could not find the right tool, so I borrowed a Phillips screwdriver from my neighbor.

senses.definition: a tool with a cross-shaped tip used for turning screws with a Pozidriv head, providing enhanced torque transfer and reduced risk of slippage

examples.x: The worker used a Pozidriv screwdriver to tighten the screws on the wooden frame.
I need a Pozidriv screwdriver to assemble this piece of furniture.
The technician grabbed a Pozidriv screwdriver to secure the electrical panel.
He reached for the Pozidriv screwdriver to finish installing the shelving unit.

senses.definition: a tool with a star-shaped tip used for turning screws with a Torx head, offering secure and efficient fastening

examples.x: For this project, you will need a Torx screwdriver to secure the parts properly.
He was able to finish the repair quickly thanks to his Torx screwdriver.
The technician grabbed a Torx screwdriver to tighten the screws on the computer motherboard.
The mechanic used a Torx screwdriver to remove the screws from the car engine.

senses.definition: a tool with a square-shaped tip used for turning screws with a square-drive head

examples.x: The carpenter used a Robertson screwdriver to secure the wood panels together.
The set of tools included a Robertson screwdriver, perfect for assembling the furniture.
The square tip of a Robertson screwdriver makes it less likely to slip during use.
For this project, the builder recommended using a Robertson screwdriver for better control and durability.
He reached for a Robertson screwdriver to fix the loose screws on the door handle.

senses.definition: a tool with a tip featuring three triangular wings, used for turning screws with a Tri-Wing head, often found in tamper-resistant applications

examples.x: The technician used a tri-wing screwdriver to open the back of the gaming console.
To repair the camera, she needed a tri-wing screwdriver to remove the screws.
He grabbed a tri-wing screwdriver to take apart the remote control for a battery replacement.
She carefully used the tri-wing screwdriver to avoid damaging the delicate parts of the gadget.

senses.definition: a tool with a two-pin or two-hole tip used for turning screws with spanner or snake eye heads, commonly used in tamper-resistant applications

examples.x: The technician used a spanner screwdriver to remove the tamper-proof screws on the machine.
When assembling the locked display case, the workers used a spanner screwdriver to secure the screws.
The security team used a spanner screwdriver to inspect the equipment without leaving any evidence of tampering.
The spanner screwdriver fits perfectly into the two small holes on the screw's head, allowing for easy removal.
After searching through the toolbox, he finally found the spanner screwdriver to fix the machinery.

senses.definition: a tool that applies a predetermined amount of torque to screws, enabling precise and controlled tightening

examples.x: The technician used a torque screwdriver to ensure the screws were tightened to the correct specifications.
To avoid damaging the circuit board, she carefully adjusted the torque screwdriver before turning the screws.
He set the torque screwdriver to 10 Newton-meters before tightening the bolts on the engine.
The torque screwdriver clicked when it reached the preset limit, indicating the screw was secure.
Using a torque screwdriver prevents over-tightening and ensures the screw is fastened properly.

senses.definition: a collection of small-sized screwdrivers with interchangeable tips, used for delicate and precise tasks in electronics, watches, jewelry, and other fine mechanics

examples.x: He used a precision screwdriver set to open the back panel of his phone.
The technician carefully selected the right tool from the precision screwdriver set to fix the watch.
She purchased a precision screwdriver set to handle the delicate screws in her computer.
A good precision screwdriver set is crucial when working on intricate projects like model building.
He needed the precision screwdriver set to adjust the screws on his glasses.

senses.definition: a tool with a ratcheting mechanism that enables easy and efficient screwdriving without the need to reposition the tool for each turn

examples.x: The ratchet screwdriver made the task of assembling furniture much faster and easier.
She preferred using a ratchet screwdriver for the car repair because it allowed for quicker adjustments.
With the ratchet screwdriver, he was able to finish the project in half the time it would have taken with a standard screwdriver.

senses.definition: a tool specifically designed with an insulated handle to provide electrical safety when working with live electrical components or circuits, reducing the risk of electrical shock or short circuits

examples.x: The electrician used an insulated screwdriver to safely tighten the screws on the live circuit.
When working with electrical panels, always ensure you have an insulated screwdriver to avoid accidents.
I need to grab my insulated screwdriver before I start fixing this appliance to stay safe.
The set of tools I bought includes an insulated screwdriver for handling electrical tasks.

senses.definition: a tool with a magnetized tip that attracts and holds screws, allowing for easier and more convenient handling and placement of screws during installation or removal, reducing the risk of dropping or losing them

examples.x: The magnetic screwdriver made it much easier to work with the tiny screws in the electronics.
I used a magnetic screwdriver to attach the handle to the door, preventing the screws from falling.
He reached for the magnetic screwdriver to fix the loose panel without worrying about dropping any screws.
A magnetic screwdriver is a must-have tool when assembling furniture to keep screws secure.

senses.definition: a flat piece of metal with a hole in the middle through which a bolt is put to secure or fasten objects together

examples.x: He tightened the nut onto the bolt to secure the bracket firmly in place.
The mechanic used a wrench to loosen the nut holding the wheel hub.
She replaced the missing nut on the bolt to prevent the assembly from coming loose.

senses.definition: a flat rubber, plastic, or metal ring which is small and acts as a seal or is put between a nut and a bolt to tighten their connection

examples.x: He used a washer to secure the bolt on the lawnmower.
The washer helps prevent leaks in the plumbing system.
She replaced the old washer to fix the dripping faucet.
The set included various sizes of washers for different projects.
A rubber washer is often used in plumbing to create a watertight seal.

senses.definition: a threaded fastener with a looped end that provides a secure anchor point for attaching ropes, cables, or other hardware

examples.x: The workers used an eye bolt to secure the heavy equipment to the crane.
A strong eye bolt was installed to hold the swing in place.
The contractor attached the cable to the eye bolt to lift the steel beams into position.
The eye bolt was tightened securely to prevent any movement in the rigging system.
He attached a rope to the eye bolt to pull the boat closer to the dock.

senses.definition: a metal tool that uses staples to fix paper or wood to other materials

examples.x: He used a staple gun to attach the fabric to the wooden frame.
She demonstrated how to use the staple gun safely to avoid accidents.
He carefully loaded the staple gun before starting his craft project.
The staple gun is commonly used in upholstery projects.
The manual staple gun is lightweight and easy to handle.

senses.definition: a small metal or plastic fastener used to secure electrical cables or wires to a surface, such as walls, floors, or studs

examples.x: He used a cable staple to secure the wire along the baseboard.
The electrician applied several cable staples to keep the cables neatly in place.
Make sure the cable is not pinched when using a cable staple to fasten it to the wall.
I need to buy more cable staples to finish wiring the garage.
The technician added a few cable staples to organize the messy cords under the desk.

senses.definition: a permanent fastener with a head on one end and a deformable shaft that forms a second head when joining materials together

examples.x: The aircraft's fuselage was assembled using thousands of rivets to ensure a strong and durable connection between the metal panels.
During the construction of the steel bridge, workers used heavy-duty rivets to join the beams and girders securely.
The engineer inspected each rivet to ensure they were properly fastened and that there were no signs of corrosion or loosening.
Rivets are commonly used in manufacturing because they provide a permanent and reliable fastening solution that can withstand high stresses.
To repair the damaged section of the ship's hull, the maintenance crew had to remove the old rivets and replace them with new ones.

senses.definition: a small plastic or metal device used to provide support and stability when attaching screws or fasteners to a wall
a small metal fastener or ring typically made of galvanized steel or other durable material, commonly used in upholstery, fencing, and automotive applications to secure materials together
a type of fastener consisting of a threaded rod or bolt that is embedded in concrete or masonry structures, used to secure objects or structures to a stable foundation

examples.x: The contractor used anchor bolts to secure the steel beams to the concrete foundation.
The wind turbine was fixed to the ground using large anchor bolts to prevent it from toppling.
When installing the heavy shelving unit, she made sure to use anchor bolts for added security.
The team installed anchor bolts to fasten the large equipment to the factory floor.
